title: QUnit Cheat Sheet
description: Essential QUnit commands and patterns for efficient testing.

commands.command: QUnit.test('example test', function(assert) { assert.ok(true, 'This test will pass.'); });
commands.description: Define a basic test with QUnit
commands.scenario: Define a basic test with QUnit

commands.command: QUnit.test('deepEqual test', function(assert) { assert.deepEqual({a: 1}, {a: 1}, 'Objects are equal'); });
QUnit.test('async test', function(assert) { var done = assert.async(); setTimeout(function() { assert.ok(true, 'Async test passed'); done(); }, 1000); });
QUnit.test('expect test', function(assert) { assert.expect(2); assert.ok(true, 'First assertion'); assert.ok(true, 'Second assertion'); });
commands.description: Use assert.expect to specify number of assertions
commands.scenario: Use assert.expect to specify number of assertions

commands.command: QUnit.test('promise test', function(assert) { return new Promise(function(resolve) { setTimeout(function() { assert.ok(true, 'Promise resolved'); resolve(); }, 1000); }); });
QUnit.module('module name', { beforeEach: function() { // setup code }, afterEach: function() { // teardown code } });
commands.description: Use QUnit.module for grouping tests with setup and teardown
commands.scenario: Use QUnit.module for grouping tests with setup and teardown

commands.description: Setup code before each test in a module
commands.scenario: Setup code before each test in a module

commands.command: QUnit.module('teardown example', { afterEach: function() { delete this.value; } });
commands.description: Teardown code after each test in a module
commands.scenario: Teardown code after each test in a module

commands.command: QUnit.test('throws test', function(assert) { assert.throws(function() { throw new Error('Error!'); }, 'Error!'); });
QUnit.test('notOk test', function(assert) { assert.notOk(false, 'This will pass'); });
commands.description: Use assert.notOk to assert false values
commands.scenario: Use assert.notOk to assert false values

commands.command: QUnit.test('propEqual test', function(assert) { assert.propEqual({a: 1}, {a: 1}, 'Properties are equal'); });

commands.description: Run QUnit tests in the browser
commands.scenario: Run QUnit tests in the browser

commands.description: Use npm to run QUnit tests
commands.scenario: Use npm to run QUnit tests
